title: Id::new and UTCDate::new return ClientError::Parse for caller precondition violations
description: Id::new and UTCDate::new return ClientError::Parse('Id may not be empty') when the caller passes an empty string. But ClientError::Parse is semantically 'server response could not be parsed' — it is produced by serde deserialization failures and JSON parse errors from server data. Returning Parse for a construction-time caller precondition violation is inconsistent with BearerAuth::new and BasicAuth::new, which correctly return ClientError::InvalidArgument. Callers cannot distinguish their own programming errors (empty Id construction) from server misbehavior (malformed JSON) if both return the same variant. Fix: change Id::new and UTCDate::new to return ClientError::InvalidArgument, consistent with the rest of the API surface.
